prompt: Documentary product-in-context photograph on a Romanian stone workshop bench: a horizontal slab of light beige travertine with visible open pores and small natural voids across its polished surface, positioned right of center. A stoneworker's gloved hand is using a small metal spatula to press transparent resin filler into one of the larger pores, a thin trail of wet resin catching the light against the honey-toned stone. Beside the hand, two real Tenax product bottles/tubes are grounded naturally on the bench: one labeled MASTIC LICHID (clear liquid polyester mastic) and one labeled MASTIC SEMISOLID WET (colored semisolid mastic), both with authentic on-disk packaging, no invented labels. Left third of the frame stays open and calm: soft workshop negative space, blurred stone dust and a folded cloth, natural daylight from a side window. No collage, no cutout, no floating product, no halo - one coherent single photograph. No people's faces visible (hand and forearm only). No ARDmag branding on any clothing.

rejectedVariants.reason: invented yellow/black Tenax label design, does not match authentic blue/red/white catalog packaging
